title: chassalia euchlora
intro: Chassalia euchlora, also known as the Malay tea bush, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in the treatment of digestive problems and fever. In herbal preparations, Chassalia euchlora is typically used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
